members.name: value
members.description: The value given as data when submitted with a `name`.

members.description:
Optional text used for typeahead purposes. By default the typeahead behavior will use the `.textContent` of the item.

props.name: MenuContentProps
props.resolvedType: { popupProps?: MenuPopupProps; placement?: Placement; side?: Side; sideOffset?: number; sideFlip?: boolean; align?: Align; alignOffset?: number; alignFlip?: boolean; avoidCollisions?: boolean; collisionBoundary?: Element | (Element | null)[] | null; collisionPadding?: Padding; arrowPadding?: number; hideShiftedArrow?: boolean; sticky?: 'partial' | 'always'; hideWhenDetached?: boolean; positionStrategy?: 'fixed' | 'absolute'; updatePositionStrategy?: 'always' | 'optimized'; disableUpdateOnLayoutShift?: boolean; prioritizePosition?: boolean; reference?: ReferenceElement; forceMount?: boolean; loop?: boolean }

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.

members.name: side
members.description:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.

members.name: sideOffset
members.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

members.name: sideFlip
members.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

members.name: align
members.description: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.

members.name: alignOffset
members.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

members.name: alignFlip
members.description:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.

members.name: avoidCollisions
members.description: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

members.name: collisionBoundary
members.description:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.

referencedTypes.name: Element
referencedTypes.description:
**`Element`** is the most general base class from which all element objects (i.e., objects that represent elements) in a Document inherit. It only has methods and properties common to all kinds of elements. More specific classes inherit from Element.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Element)

members.name: collisionPadding
members.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

members.name: arrowPadding
members.description:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.

members.name: hideShiftedArrow
members.description:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.

members.name: sticky
members.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

members.name: hideWhenDetached
members.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

members.name: positionStrategy
members.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

members.name: updatePositionStrategy
members.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.name: disableUpdateOnLayoutShift
members.description: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

members.name: prioritizePosition
members.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

members.name: reference
members.description:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

members.name: loop
members.description: Whether keyboard navigation should loop around

referencedTypes.name: KeyboardEvent
referencedTypes.description:
**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. The event type (keydown, keypress, or keyup) identifies what kind of keyboard activity occurred.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)

callables.name: pointerDownOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: focusOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: interactOutside
callables.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.

callables.name: openAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.

members.name: replace
members.description: Calls `router.replace` instead of `router.push`.

members.name: href

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, the link is disabled.

members.name: activeClass
members.description: Class to apply when the link is active

members.name: exactActiveClass
members.description: Class to apply when the link is exact active

members.name: inactiveClass

members.name: prefetchedClass
members.description: A class to apply to links that have been prefetched.

members.name: external
members.description: Forces the link to be considered as external (true) or internal (false). This is helpful to handle edge-cases

members.description: Pass the returned promise of `router.push()` to `document.startViewTransition()` if supported.

members.name: target
members.description: Where to display the linked URL, as the name for a browsing context.

members.name: rel
members.description: A rel attribute value to apply on the link. Defaults to "noopener noreferrer" for external links.

members.name: noRel
members.description: If set to true, no rel attribute will be added to the link

members.name: prefetch
members.description: When enabled will prefetch middleware, layouts and payloads of links in the viewport.

members.name: prefetchOn
members.description: Allows controlling when to prefetch links. By default, prefetch is triggered only on visibility.

members.name: noPrefetch
members.description: Escape hatch to disable `prefetch` attribute.

members.name: trailingSlash
members.description:
An option to either add or remove trailing slashes in the `href` for this specific link.
Overrides the global `trailingSlash` option if provided.

members.name: disabled

Disable teleport and render the component inline

Defer the resolving of a Teleport target until other parts of the application have mounted (requires Vue 3.5.0+)

{@link https://vuejs.org/guide/built-ins/teleport.html#deferred-teleport}

members.name: to
members.description: Route Location the link should navigate to when clicked on.

members.name: replace
members.description: Calls `router.replace` instead of `router.push`.

members.name: href
members.description: The URL the link should navigate to when clicked on.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, the link is disabled.

members.name: activeClass
members.description: Class to apply when the link is active

members.name: exactActiveClass
members.description: Class to apply when the link is exact active

members.name: inactiveClass
members.description: The class to apply to the link when it is inactive.

members.name: prefetchedClass
members.description: A class to apply to links that have been prefetched.

members.name: external
members.description: Forces the link to be considered as external (true) or internal (false). This is helpful to handle edge-cases

members.name: ariaCurrentValue
members.description: Value passed to the attribute `aria-current` when the link is exact active.

members.name: viewTransition
members.description: Pass the returned promise of `router.push()` to `document.startViewTransition()` if supported.

members.name: target
members.description: Where to display the linked URL, as the name for a browsing context.

members.name: rel
members.description: A rel attribute value to apply on the link. Defaults to "noopener noreferrer" for external links.

members.name: noRel
members.description: If set to true, no rel attribute will be added to the link

members.name: prefetch
members.description: When enabled will prefetch middleware, layouts and payloads of links in the viewport.

members.name: prefetchOn
members.description: Allows controlling when to prefetch links. By default, prefetch is triggered only on visibility.

members.name: noPrefetch
members.description: Escape hatch to disable `prefetch` attribute.

members.name: trailingSlash
members.description:
An option to either add or remove trailing slashes in the `href` for this specific link.
Overrides the global `trailingSlash` option if provided.

members.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

members.name: as
members.description: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwrite by `asChild`

referencedTypes.name: MenuSubContentProps
referencedTypes.resolvedType: { loop?: boolean; placement?: Placement; popupProps?: MenuPopupProps; sideOffset?: number; arrowPadding?: number; updatePositionStrategy?: 'always' | 'optimized'; sideFlip?: boolean; alignOffset?: number; alignFlip?: boolean; avoidCollisions?: boolean; collisionBoundary?: Element | (Element | null)[] | null; collisionPadding?: Padding; hideShiftedArrow?: boolean; sticky?: 'partial' | 'always'; hideWhenDetached?: boolean; positionStrategy?: 'fixed' | 'absolute'; disableUpdateOnLayoutShift?: boolean; prioritizePosition?: boolean; reference?: ReferenceElement; forceMount?: boolean }

members.name: loop
members.description: Whether keyboard navigation should loop around

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.

members.name: sideOffset
members.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

members.name: arrowPadding
members.description:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.

members.name: updatePositionStrategy
members.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.name: sideFlip
members.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

members.name: alignOffset
members.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

members.name: alignFlip
members.description:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.

members.name: avoidCollisions
members.description: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

members.name: collisionBoundary
members.description:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.

members.name: collisionPadding
members.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

members.name: hideShiftedArrow
members.description:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.

members.name: sticky
members.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

members.name: hideWhenDetached
members.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

members.name: positionStrategy
members.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

members.name: disableUpdateOnLayoutShift
members.description: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

members.name: prioritizePosition
members.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

members.name: reference
members.description:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

members.name: orientation
members.description:
Orientation of the component.

Either `vertical` or `horizontal`. Defaults to `horizontal`.

members.name: decorative
members.description:
Whether or not the component is purely decorative.
When `true`, accessibility-related attributes are updated so that that the rendered element is removed from the accessibility tree.

members.name: symbolize
members.description: Whether to convert the command value to symbol representation.

referencedTypes.name: KeyboardEvent
referencedTypes.description:
**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. The event type (keydown, keypress, or keyup) identifies what kind of keyboard activity occurred.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)

callables.name: pointerDownOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: focusOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: interactOutside
callables.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.

content will be visible to screen readers.

callables.name: pointerDownOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: focusOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: interactOutside
callables.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.

callables.name: openAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.

callables.name: closeAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on close. Can be prevented.

props.name: MenuSubTriggerProps
props.resolvedType: { disabled?: boolean; textValue?: string; asChild?: boolean; as?: AsTag | Component }

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with the item.

members.name: textValue
members.description:
Optional text used for typeahead purposes. By default the typeahead behavior will use the `.textContent` of the item.
Use this when the content is complex, or you have non-textual content inside.

members.name: asChild
members.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

members.name: as
members.description: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwrite by `asChild`

referencedTypes.name: Component
referencedTypes.description:
A type used in public APIs where a component type is expected.
The constructor type is an artificial type returned by defineComponent().

props.name: MenuTriggerProps
props.resolvedType: { disabled?: boolean; asChild?: boolean; as?: AsTag | Component }

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, the menu would not open when triggered.

members.name: asChild
members.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

members.name: as
members.description: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwrite by `asChild`

referencedTypes.name: Component
referencedTypes.description:
A type used in public APIs where a component type is expected.
The constructor type is an artificial type returned by defineComponent().
